items.content:
[The content of this article has been produced by our advertising partner.]
The enterprise software landscape is undergoing a structural reckoning. According to recent market data, AI-native spending has surged by 94% year-on-year (YoY), nearly double the growth rate of hybrid tools and dramatically outpacing traditional SaaS, which has cooled to just 8%. Amidst this shift, omnichannel leader Omnichat today announces its definitive evolution into an AI-Native Agentic Customer Experience (CX)...

items.title: Supersized and scaling: China pushes 10,000-card computing clusters in AI race
items.creator: Ann Cao
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Supersized and scaling: China pushes 10,000-card computing clusters in AI race
items.content:
In China, computing facilities have emerged as a new form of infrastructure over the past two years, sparking an arms race among cities and technology companies to build 10,000-card computing clusters.
These clusters – which link 10,000 or more artificial intelligence accelerator chips – enable faster iteration of AI capabilities and significantly reduce model training times.
Domestic champions, from tech giants such as Huawei Technologies and Alibaba Group Holding to graphics processing unit...

items.title: Samsung reshuffle signals shift beyond hardware as China rivalry intensifies
items.creator: Yeon Woo Lee
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Samsung reshuffle signals shift beyond hardware as China rivalry intensifies
items.content:
Samsung Electronics’ surprise leadership reshuffle in its TV division points to a strategic pivot away from a hardware-led model, analysts have said, as intensifying competition from Chinese rivals pushes the company to rethink how it generates revenue beyond television sales.
The South Korean tech giant on Monday appointed Lee Won-jin, former head of its global marketing office, to lead the visual display business.
Samsung said Lee was “expected to spearhead business turnarounds and identify...

items.title: Hype or real cash flow: China’s robot boom faces reality check as commercialisation lags
items.creator: Wency Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hype or real cash flow: China’s robot boom faces reality check as commercialisation lags
items.content:
A desk with a laptop and a stack of books. A shirt waiting to be folded. A kitchen counter, a milk-tea stand and pile of building blocks – all in a single room. Robots of different shapes face their tasks with focus, controlled by a human operator.
Pick up. Place. Fold. Repeat.
This is not a film set, it is a data collection factory for embodied intelligence – a de facto “data foundry”.
The facility, in Shanghai’s Zhangjiang hi-tech zone, is run by Agibot, a fast-rising robotics company. The...

items.title: China’s robots step into real-world roles, from cleaning to directing traffic
items.creator: Eunice Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s robots step into real-world roles, from cleaning to directing traffic
items.content:
Robots are beginning to reshape everyday life in China as the country accelerates its embrace of embodied artificial intelligence, handling tasks from cleaning homes and directing traffic to repairing equipment in hazardous factory settings.
Embodied AI refers to AI embedded in physical machines, such as robots, that can sense their environment, make decisions and act in the real world.
A cleaning service that launched in March on 58.com, a Chinese classifieds platform, pairs a human cleaner...

items.title: How US tech hegemony is locking out the Global South
items.creator: Zhou Xiaoming
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: How US tech hegemony is locking out the Global South

images.alt: Activists protest at the World Trade Organization headquarters urging governments to demand waivers for Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS) amid the Covid-19 pandemic, in Geneva, Switzerland, on June 15, 2022. Photo: EPA-EFE
images.title: Activists protest at the World Trade Organization headquarters urging governments to demand waivers for Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS) amid the Covid-19 pandemic, in Geneva, Switzerland, on June 15, 2022. Photo: EPA-EFE

items.content:
In global discourse, a script has been handed to us: the United States and China are locked in a “tech race”. But this is really a misnomer. True competition requires a level playing field. When one runner trips the other to ensure victory, it’s not a competition; it’s cheating.
So, when Washington deploys an arsenal of sanctions, export controls and diplomatic strong-arming to hamstring China’s technological ascent, it is not competing. It is an act of suppression.
This reflects a deliberate...

items.content:
[The content of this article has been produced by our advertising partner.]
More than four million users now access OpenAI’s coding assistant Codex weekly, a recent uptake of one million users in two weeks proving just how quickly the competitive gap in developer tools is widening. Recognising this urgency, Amazon Web Services (AWS) has brought Codex and the latest artificial intelligence (AI) models to Amazon Bedrock. For CIOs and CTOs, this means capturing the fruits of agentic AI within...

items.title: Hong Kong puts its own spin on DeepSeek with China-chip AI push abroad
items.creator: Xinmei Shen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hong Kong puts its own spin on DeepSeek with China-chip AI push abroad
items.content:
A Hong Kong government-backed artificial intelligence lab is preparing to launch a new model built on DeepSeek that can run entirely on Chinese-made chips.
The Hong Kong Generative AI Research and Development Centre (HKGAI) plans to unveil its HKGAI-V3 model in the first half of this year as it looks to export its “sovereign AI” capabilities to overseas markets.
The system was based on DeepSeek V4 architecture with “full-parameter fine tuning for localisation”, and had been optimised to operate...

items.title: China, Indonesia launch cross-border QR payments – a boost for the global yuan?
items.creator: Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China, Indonesia launch cross-border QR payments – a boost for the global yuan?
items.content:
China and Indonesia have launched a new cross-border QR payment system, marking the latest step in Beijing’s drive to build a regional digital payments network as it pushes to internationalise the yuan and reduce dependence on the US dollar.
Users can now use domestic mobile apps – such as China’s Alipay and Indonesia’s QRIS – to scan QR codes and make retail payments in either country using their home currencies.
Alicia Garcia-Herrero, chief economist for the Asia-Pacific region at French...

items.title: Energy crisis showcases strengths of China’s data centre market
items.creator: Nicholas Spiro
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Energy crisis showcases strengths of China’s data centre market
items.content:
Earlier this year, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang likened artificial intelligence (AI) to “a five-layer cake”. At the top are applications, such as chatbots. The layer below is the software, which includes language models. Further down are infrastructure and memory chips.
The bottom layer is the most important. “At the foundation is energy,” Huang said, noting that “energy is the first principle of AI infrastructure and the binding constraint on how much intelligence the system can produce”.
Driving...

items.title: AI cost-cutting not a legal excuse to fire workers, Chinese court says
items.creator: Eunice Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: AI cost-cutting not a legal excuse to fire workers, Chinese court says
items.content:
A court in China has ruled it illegal for a company to terminate an employee on the grounds that an artificial intelligence replacement would be cheaper, affirming limits on AI-driven job displacement amid a wave of anxiety over the technology’s potential to fuel unemployment.
A 35-year-old worker surnamed Zhou who oversaw AI-generated responses at a fintech firm in Hangzhou, capital of east China’s Zhejiang province, was fired after refusing a demotion and pay cut. The company told him his role...

images.title: DeepSeek says its V4 model delivered top-tier performance in coding benchmarks and showed significant advantages in reasoning and agentic tasks. Photo: Reuters

items.content:
DeepSeek’s launch of its latest artificial intelligence model could likely trigger a broad reassessment of stocks across the industry chain from chipmakers to large language model developers, analysts say, with the breakthrough poised to drive demand for computing power and more commercial adoption.
The unveiling of the V4 series marked another milestone for the Hangzhou-based start-up, which described the model as the most powerful open-source platform capable of challenging US rivals such as...

items.title: From footwear to AI services: China moves up the value chain in exports
items.creator: Coco Feng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: From footwear to AI services: China moves up the value chain in exports

images.alt: An automated production line at Zhejiang XCC Group in Shaoxing, east China’s Zhejiang province, March 30, 2026. Photo: Xinhua
images.title: An automated production line at Zhejiang XCC Group in Shaoxing, east China’s Zhejiang province, March 30, 2026. Photo: Xinhua

items.content:
For decades, the global image of Chinese trade was synonymous with massive container ships loaded with footwear, bags and furniture. However, a profound structural shift is under way.
Having conquered the world of physical goods, China is now rapidly ascending the industrial chain as an emerging exporter of manufacturing technology.
In 2025, the country’s exports of telecoms, computer and information services reached 808 billion yuan (US$118 billion), marking a robust 13 per cent year-on-year...

items.title: World may find itself ‘in a very Chinese time’ of data governance
items.creator: Ran Guo
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: World may find itself ‘in a very Chinese time’ of data governance
items.content:
At the end of March, China inaugurated the World Data Organisation in Beijing, a body with a stated mission of “bridging the data divide, unlocking data’s value and powering the digital economy”.
The move is the latest signal of a broader trend: over the past several years, Beijing has developed a distinct data governance strategy to drive artificial intelligence (AI) development as it reshapes the terms of technological competition.
Since late 2025, Beijing has pursued an aggressive AI adoption...

items.title: From phones to robots: China’s supply chain eyes next growth curve in humanoid (原标题: From phones to humanoid robots: China’s supply chain eyes next growth curve)
items.creator: Iris Deng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: From phones to robots: China’s supply chain eyes next growth curve in humanoid
items.content:
China’s smartphone and electronics supply chain is adapting its expertise to support the country’s fledgling but fast-growing humanoid robotics industry, as component suppliers seek new growth beyond a slowing mobile market.
The sector received a glimpse of that crossover after Honor’s humanoid robot D1, a dark-horse entrant from the smartphone maker, won Beijing’s recent robot half-marathon, beating established Chinese robotics names such as Unitree.
Honor entered the humanoid robotics sector...

items.title: Beyond subsidies: what’s really driving China’s industrial climb
items.creator: Zongshuai Fan
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Beyond subsidies: what’s really driving China’s industrial climb

images.alt: University students attend a job fair in Fuyang, Anhui province, on April 28. Photo: CN-STR / AFP
images.title: University students attend a job fair in Fuyang, Anhui province, on April 28. Photo: CN-STR / AFP

items.content:
Driven by intensifying competition in advanced manufacturing, the world is waking up to “China shock 2.0”. The first “shock”, associated with China’s accession to the World Trade Organization in 2001, focused on low-tech manufacturing.
Of course, this latest “shock” isn’t framed as such by Chinese policymakers. They present it as an upgrade in export strength, often described as a shift from the “old three” of textiles, furniture and home appliances to the “new three” of electric vehicles,...

images.alt: ByteDance’s Anew Labs lists 36 core members, as well as big names on its scientific advisory board. Photo: Shutterstock
images.title: ByteDance’s Anew Labs lists 36 core members, as well as big names on its scientific advisory board. Photo: Shutterstock

items.content:
ByteDance, the Chinese owner of TikTok, is pushing deeper into artificial intelligence with Anew Labs, a drug-discovery unit that has begun presenting its AI-designed therapies at international conferences.
The unit – also known as Anew Therapeutics or ByteDance AI Drug Discovery – operates from Shanghai, Singapore and San Jose, California.
Its official website listed 36 core members, as well as big names on its “scientific advisory board”: Liu Yongjun, former president of Innovent Biologics, Ji...

items.title: Comeback trail: can foreign marques regain lost ground amid China’s EV revolution?
items.creator: Daniel Ren,Themis Qi

items.translated_title: Comeback trail: can foreign marques regain lost ground amid China’s EV revolution?

images.alt: A Hyundai Ioniq electric vehicle cut in half is on display at the Auto China show. Photo: Getty Images
images.title: A Hyundai Ioniq electric vehicle cut in half is on display at the Auto China show. Photo: Getty Images

items.content:
At the close of his presentation at this year’s Auto China show, Li Fenggang, president of Beijing Hyundai Motor, surprised the audience by asking them to lift LED boxes placed on their seats.
“We’ll use this beam of light to kick off Hyundai’s new journey in China,” he said, raising the device stamped with the logo of Ioniq, the new brand the Korean carmaker has just launched in mainland China.
After years of lacklustre performance in the country, Hyundai was preparing to regain lost ground by...

images.alt: Steve Sun, founding managing partner of Dalton Venture. Photo: Handout

items.content:
Hong Kong has made “significant” progress in its bid to transform itself into a technology hub, according to a Chinese venture capital investor, whose firm was recently selected by the city to invest in local start-ups under a HK$2 billion (US$250 million) government initiative.
“You can see fundamental, significant changes are indeed taking place,” said Steve Sun, founding managing partner of Dalton Venture, in an interview on Thursday. “I believe that over the next decade, Hong Kong can...

items.title: No-drone zone: DJI, consumers grapple with Beijing sales ban and registration rule
items.creator: Ben Jiang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: No-drone zone: DJI, consumers grapple with Beijing sales ban and registration rule
items.content:
A sweeping rule change for drones in Beijing, which bars sales in the Chinese capital and requires identity registration linked to each device from Friday, has left sellers and consumers scrambling to cope with some of the country’s toughest regulations on the sector.
At a flagship retail store operated by DJI, the world’s largest drone maker, in Beijing’s bustling Guomao area, a shop assistant said that the store had been preparing to clear its shelves of all drone products in accordance with...
